gauges.name: Wisconsin River at Rothschild
gauges.description: Primary gauge for the Wausau metro area

tips.Walleye: Jigs tipped with minnows near current breaks and dam tailwaters. Best at dawn/dusk. Try 1/4 oz jig heads bounced along sandy bottoms.
tips.Smallmouth Bass: Crankbaits and tube jigs around rocky shorelines and bridge pilings. Topwater action in summer evenings.
tips.Musky: Large bucktails and jerkbaits along weed edges and deep structure. Fall is prime — focus on figure-8s at the boat.
tips.Channel Catfish: Cut bait or stink bait on the bottom near deeper holes. Night fishing in summer is most productive.
tips.Sturgeon: Catch-and-release only. Observe from shore during the spring spawning run — a spectacular sight near Rothschild dam.

season_notes: Walleye/Sauger open first Sat in May

access_points.name: Rothschild Boat Landing
access_points.directions: Off River Dr, east of Hwy 51 bridge

tips.Walleye: Work the Mosinee dam tailrace with jigs and live bait. Current seams hold fish especially in spring.
tips.Smallmouth Bass: Crawfish-pattern crankbaits and ned rigs along the rocky banks below the dam.
tips.Musky: Trolling large crankbaits through the deeper pools downstream. Look for baitfish schools on electronics.
tips.Channel Catfish: Chicken liver or nightcrawlers fished on bottom rigs in the slower pools below the dam.

season_notes: Walleye/Sauger open first Sat in May

access_points.name: Mosinee Boat Landing
access_points.directions: Off Main St near the Mosinee dam

gauges.name: Big Rib River at Rib Falls

tips.Brook Trout: Small spinners (#0–#2 Mepps) and worms in the upper Class I reaches. Wade quietly upstream — brookies spook easily.
tips.Brown Trout: Larger streamers and Rapala minnows in the deeper pools. Best fishing is early morning or after dark in summer.
tips.Smallmouth Bass: Small crankbaits and soft plastics in the lower reaches near Rib Falls. Look for them near log jams.

regulations.rule: Special regs on some upstream reaches

season_notes: Early catch-and-release trout opens first Sat in Jan
Regular season first Sat in May

access_points.name: Rib Falls Dam
access_points.directions: County Rd N at Rib Falls

gauges.name: Little Rib River near Wausau
gauges.description: Tributary flowing through western Marathon County

tips.Brook Trout: Ultra-light gear with small spinners or live worms. A small, brushy stream — short casts and stealth are key. Best in spring and early summer.

season_notes: Early catch-and-release trout opens first Sat in Jan
Regular season first Sat in May

access_points.name: Cty Rd J Crossing
access_points.directions: County Rd J west of Wausau

gauges.name: Eau Claire River near Kelly
gauges.description: Major eastern tributary of the Wisconsin River

tips.Smallmouth Bass: Tube jigs and soft plastic crawfish along rocky runs. The Eau Claire has excellent wade fishing for bronzebacks.
tips.Walleye: Jig and minnow combos in deeper pools near Kelly dam. Evening and night fishing most productive.
tips.Brown Trout: Found in the cooler upper reaches. Drift nymphs or swing wet flies through the riffles.

season_notes: Trout regs apply on upper reaches

access_points.name: Kelly Dam Landing
access_points.directions: Off Cty Rd Y near Kelly

gauges.name: Big Eau Pleine River at Stratford
gauges.description: Feeds the Eau Pleine Reservoir southwest of Wausau

tips.Walleye: Jig and minnow near the reservoir inlet in spring. Troll crankbaits along drop-offs in summer.
tips.Northern Pike: Spinnerbaits and large spoons along weed edges. Spring pike stack up near creek inlets after ice-out.
tips.Panfish: Small jigs tipped with wax worms near submerged timber. Excellent ice fishing for crappie and bluegill in winter.
tips.Largemouth Bass: Texas-rigged soft plastics and spinnerbaits around fallen trees and lily pad edges in summer.

season_notes: Walleye open first Sat in May
Reservoir influence — good ice fishing in winter

access_points.name: Eau Pleine County Park
access_points.directions: Off Cty Rd HH south of Stratford

reservoirs.name: Rainbow Reservoir
reservoirs.description: Controls upper WI River flow north of Wausau

reservoirs.name: Willow Reservoir
reservoirs.description: Regulates Willow Creek into the WI River

reservoirs.name: Spirit Reservoir
reservoirs.description: Feeds Spirit River — affects WI River levels

reservoirs.name: Eau Pleine Reservoir
reservoirs.description: Directly feeds Big Eau Pleine gauge at Stratford

reservoirs.name: Rice Reservoir
reservoirs.description: Controls Rice Creek flow into the WI River

seasonal_activity.label: March
activity: Ice-out approaching — use caution on ice
Walleye pre-spawn staging in river mouths
Suckers running — good live bait source

upcoming_events.name: Inland Trout Harvest Opener
upcoming_events.description: NEW for 2026 — harvest season opens a full month earlier than prior years. Streams, springs, and spring ponds. Requires inland trout stamp.

upcoming_events.name: Governor's Fishing Opener
upcoming_events.description: Annual tradition since 1966. Family Fishing Day on May 2 at Lake Hayward Beach with casting lessons, DNR Fishmobile, and giveaways.

upcoming_events.name: General Inland Fishing Opener
upcoming_events.description: Walleye, bass (harvest), northern pike, and musky all open statewide. Musky opener now unified to May 2 (previously Memorial Day weekend for northern zone).

upcoming_events.name: Lake & Pond Trout Season Opens
upcoming_events.description: Trout season on inland lakes and ponds. The earlier April 4 opener applies only to streams and springs.

upcoming_events.name: Free Fishing Weekend
upcoming_events.description: June 6–7. No license, trout stamp, or salmon stamp required for residents or nonresidents. All bag/size limits still apply. Great for families!

conditions_summary: Flows dropping on WI River — Rothschild (-20%), Big Rib River (-53%), Big Eau Pleine (-65%) — clarity improving.
